title: Fix --build-args handling
body:
When passing an environment variable to a Containerfile using an `ARG` command,
the dry-run processing  for `COPY` and `ADD` isn't resolving the environment
variable for the source and is causing a failure.

Now convert the source if it's an environment variable to its value and then
proceed on.  The destination doesn't have a similar issue at this point 
but we'll convert it too just in case.

Addresses: #1871

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@nalind I looked for a while today, but couldn't come up with a quick way to resolve the destination for the dry run if it was an environment variable.  I'll look more tomorrow. If you've suggestions, happy to take them.

@mheon, @rhatdan we may want to push to at least get this stop gap fix in for 1.6.
I take it buildah has not translate $build_arg internally at this point?
@rhatdan, not that I could find quickly.  I was going to dive in again Monday, but think it's down in imagebuilder.

Would having the callers of `StageExecutor.digestSpecifiedContent()` pass `ib.Arguments()` in as an additional argument, and have `digestSpecifiedContent()` use that slice and `imagebuilder.ProcessWord()` to expand source and destination values, produce the right results?
@nalind, maybe, I'll take a look.  Thanks for the suggestion/pointers.
Assuming happy tests, ready to ship.  @nalind TYVM for the excellent tips/pointers.  Spot on as usual.
FYI @mheon
This is too late for Podman 1.6.0, but we have some important rootless bugs, so we'll probably be cutting a 1.6.1 later this week anyways.
We'll probably want to expand variables in the destination, in case it includes one, around line 347.
My testing didn't show that I needed to add the check on the destination too, but maybe that was luck?  I'll ProcessWord(destination.Value) just above the line you suggested to be ultra sure.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably e2c33f3) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Touch up with @nalind's latest suggestion/comment and have rebased.    Finger's crossed for the CI.
Your tests have a bug in them.
@rhatdan, I'd accidentally remove the '}' from the prior test when I cut/pasted the new ones in.  Ugh.

LGTM and I like the tests.
@nalind Please take one final look.
LGTM
Happy green test buttons and two LGTM's if someone wants to merge this in...
@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit 51a6421 has been approved by `nalind`
